title: I work at a company that is looking to switch platforms from Workfront to Monday.com, has anyone here made that swithc?
excerpt: Hello! I am the internal admin for our company’s Workfront instance. We are looking to make the switch to a new platform but we have some users who are unsire of Monday’s ability to meet their needs. Does anyone here hav…

posts.author: tvasilovic (Tim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! I am the internal admin for our company’s Workfront instance. We are looking to make the switch to a new platform but we have some users who are unsire of Monday’s ability to meet their needs. Does anyone here have experience with Workfront and swithing over to Monday.com who might be willing to share insight and experience?
Thanks!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve never used workfront but I’ve looked into it in the past. Can I ask what made you all look at a new product? ie what is it about workfront that is prompting you to change? And what do you all like about workfront? I can give a better answer.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve never used workfront but I have migrated companies from platforms like Asana and Basecamp to monday.com and the first question that I always ask, is “what is the challenging you are facing with the current platform and how do you expect monday.com to solve those?”

So it is very important to understand the why of the transition to see if the new product/service is what you need. However, as a 7+ year monday.com user, I’ve seen the platform adapting and exceeding expectations from many teams when comparing to what they had before.
